steps.title: Use the slope formula
steps.content:
The fastest approach is to apply the slope formula directly:
$$m = \frac{y_2 - y_1}{x_2 - x_1}$$
This formula calculates the ratio of vertical change to horizontal change between two points.

steps.title: Substitute the points
steps.content:
With points $(5, 7)$ and $(16, 26)$:
$$m = \frac{26 - 7}{16 - 5} = \frac{19}{11}$$
The slope of the line is $\frac{19}{11}$.
The answer is 19/11.
